--- Thread (1 messages, 2020-06-07) ---
[2020-06-07 18:17] .bky_1556 (bky): > people pay 4R to make their engineers prompt
You mean electrovores? Most of the 6R for a fresh Electrovore/Engineer pair is for the attack (worth roughly 5R). 

A small amount of its value is the option to sacrifice the pair on defense like a Steelsplitter (nothing early, but up to 1 gold at peak) or absorb for 1 instead of attacking (roughly 2/5 of a gold in mono-red for the first one). 

The residue, 0-1 gold depending on circumstance, is the actual cost of making engineers prompt.

[2020-06-08 16:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Hmm I don't recall a set where I felt Savior made it player favored
[2020-06-08 16:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Savior results in many interesting games for me
[2020-06-08 16:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): It's not always mirrored
[2020-06-08 16:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Many times it's refuted successfully or unsuccessfully
[2020-06-08 16:34] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Or is that not meant to happen in high level play?
[2020-06-08 16:34] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I.e. at high level nobody will go Savior if it can be refuted, so those games never happen?
[2020-06-08 16:34] p0lari (Polari): I find it's usually (though not quite always) mirrored and rarely ends up close
[2020-06-08 16:35] p0lari (Polari): but savior games are crazy and it's impossible to say if it was player advantage in the set or just one side making better decisions
[2020-06-08 16:36] p0lari (Polari): though the first savior does feel like a big advantage just like first wincer/lucina/whathaveyou
[2020-06-08 16:36] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Due to the high amount of resources, I think Savior is a high-skill unit
[2020-06-08 16:36] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Cos there's more space for a stronger player to make better decisions
[2020-06-08 16:36] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): In tech composition, attackers, defense
[2020-06-08 16:36] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And when to stop clicking
[2020-06-08 16:36] p0lari (Polari): yeah the tech often ends up asymmetric

[2020-06-08 16:37] jamberine: and then there are a bunch of fixed turns
[2020-06-08 16:37] jamberine: so p2 should be behind by a good amount by the time thing are happening

[2020-06-08 16:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Here's from Msven's video
[2020-06-08 16:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): ```
P1 t3: DD DD DD1 DD DDE DDD DDDEE DDDDD (t7 14 spare, t8 29 spare, t9 67 spare)
P1 t4: DD DDE DDD DD1 DDDE DDDD DDDD D (t7 4 spare, t8 41 spare, t9 81 spare)
P2 t3: DD DD DD1 DDE DDDE DDDD DDDD D (t7 20 spare, t8 55 spare, t9 93 spare)
P2 t4: DD DDE DDD DDDE1 DDDD DDDD DD (t7 16 spare, t8 58 spare, t9 98 spare)
```

p1 vs p2
67 spare vs 93 spare gold on t9
